frameworks.name: rational inattention
frameworks.one_line: Agents pay cognitive cost to increase precision, scaled by average reward

frameworks.name: average reward theory of tonic dopamine
frameworks.one_line: Tonic DA reports average reward availability driving response vigor

frameworks.name: Bayesian theory of dopamine as precision
frameworks.one_line: Tonic DA controls the precision weighting of inference

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Domain in which positive/negative feedback asymmetries are predicted

core_question: Can rational inattention reconcile the average-reward and precision accounts of tonic dopamine without crosstalk?
key_assumptions: Average reward and precision are monotonically linked
Agents can purchase precision by paying a cognitive cost
Tonic DA reports the average reward signal
A single DA signal can serve both functions via this link
manipulations: Conceptual variation of tonic DA state (high vs low)
Timing of DA change (learning vs performance phase)
key_findings: Framework conceptually links average reward and precision
Predicts enhanced learning from positive feedback under high DA and negative feedback under low DA
Predicts DA tips exploration-exploitation balance toward exploitation
Predicts DA speeds the internal clock and reduces central-tendency interference in temporal reproduction
interpretations: Rational inattention unifies the average-reward and precision theories of tonic DA under one mechanism
Monotonic average-reward-to-precision mapping lets a single DA signal carry both pieces of information
